question.text: When does a monthly currency futures contract expire?

answer.text: Two working days prior to the last business day of the expiry month at 12:30 PM
On the last business day of the expiry month at 5:00 PM
On the last Thursday of the expiry month at 3:30 PM
One working day before the last business day at 12:30 PM

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Monthly currency futures expire two working days prior to the last business day of the expiry month at 12:30 PM (not the regular close of 5:00 PM or 7:30 PM). **The Trap:** The last Thursday rule applies to equity derivatives (Nifty/Bank Nifty), not currency derivatives. **Cross-Exam Context:** Expiry conventions differ between equity (Series VIII) and currency derivatives (Series I). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** If the last business day of April is 30th (Tuesday), the expiry date is 26th April (Friday), assuming no holidays.
